title: gynoxys miniphylla
intro: Gynoxys miniphylla, also known as the "Andean wormseed", is a plant species native to South America, particularly in the Andes mountains, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The medicinal properties of Gynoxys miniphylla include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Gynoxys miniphylla can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine in the Andean region.
